id: "13.2.5"
title: "ITSI KPI Base Search Performance, Run-Time Variance, Backfill Coverage, and Pseudo-Entity Discovery Rate (scheduler multisearch, itsi_summary freshness skew, ItsiKpiSearch daemon hints, saved_search_query_metrics REST, governance inventory join)"

criticality: "critical"

---

# UC-13.2.5 · ITSI KPI Base Search Performance, Run-Time Variance, Backfill Coverage, and Pseudo-Entity Discovery Rate (scheduler multisearch, itsi_summary freshness skew, ItsiKpiSearch daemon hints, saved_search_query_metrics REST, governance inventory join)

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Expert &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Reliability, Performance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch the big prep station that chops ingredients for many meals at once. When that station runs late, loses track of who ordered what, or starts labeling mystery bowls, every downstream dish looks wrong even if the ovens still glow green. We rank the slowest prep lanes so crews fix the foundation before diners notice cold plates.*

---

## Description

Detects Splunk ITSI KPI base searches whose scheduler runtime, skew versus peer median, skip or continued cadence, pseudo-entity rate, and summary freshness lag threaten dependent KPI states, fusing multisearch lanes with inventory and optional metadata joins to rank owner-attributed remediation paths.

## Value

Platform teams prevent silent not-available KPI tiles and false-green service views by exposing which shared base searches starve the KPI engine, when pseudo-entities flood results after discovery failure, and which bases need scope splits or cron realignment before customer journeys degrade.

## Implementation

Schedule uc_13_2_5_itsi_kpi_base_search_performance_ranked every fifteen minutes on the ITSI search head with earliest=-6h@h; forward scheduler, splunkd ITSI components, and itsi_summary; maintain itsi_base_search_inventory.csv; tune pseudo and freshness gates per tenant; page on critical or high severity with thirty-minute dedupe; archive weekly CSV exports for governance.

## Evidence

Saved search uc_13_2_5_itsi_kpi_base_search_performance_ranked with fifteen-minute schedule, exported CSV of critical and high rows joined to itsi_base_search_inventory.csv, and ticketed screenshots from Service Analyzer for the same base_search_id.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab ITSI tenant, widen earliest bounds on a non-production KPI base search until run_time_p95_sec exceeds eighty percent of the scheduled interval while itsi_summary stops updating for dependent kpi_id values, then confirm uc_13_2_5_itsi_kpi_base_search_performance_ranked returns critical within thirty minutes with non-null recommended_action.

### Negative scenario

After restoring the base search scope and scheduler cadence, when skip_cnt is zero, pseudo_entity_pct is under five percent, freshness_lag_minutes is under five, and run_time_p95_sec is below half the interval budget, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for four consecutive runs on the canary base search.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with Splunk IT Service Intelligence administrators who curate KPI templates, reliability engineers who size search heads, and service owners who accept freshness contracts for executive health tiles. This use case isolates KPI base search economics: one scheduled search feeds many KPI definitions through shared result sets, entity extraction rules, and threshold scripts. It does not replace UC-13.2.1 composite Service Health Score regression trending, UC-13.2.2 per-KPI degradation burn-rate alerting, UC-13.2.3 episode volume and mean time to resolve analytics, UC-13.2.6 rules engine fidelity, or UC-13.2.18 adaptive threshold drift. It complements UC-13.1.5 generic scheduled-search starvation by attributing pain specifically to ITSI KPI base search identities, pseudo-entity fall-through, and summary freshness that makes downstream KPI states read not available while the user interface still looks superficially green.

KPI base search architecture begins in itsi_kpi_base_search.conf style objects that bind a saved search name to a schedule, a splunk_server affinity when required, and a filter expression that scopes raw events before KPI post-processing. itsi_kpi.conf and companion knowledge link kpi_id values to base search rows, metric_qualifier fields, entity identifier columns, and unit normalization. When entity discovery lookups fail or return explosive cardinality, ITSI falls back to pseudo-entity behavior so the engine can still emit rows; the cost is mis-attribution, inflated row counts, and threshold scripts that average nonsense. Operators see not available on KPI tiles when freshness windows expire, when base searches skip, or when entity_id columns no longer join to service entities after field renames.

Entity_id extraction depends on fieldaliases, transforms, and lookup outputs that must match the column names referenced in KPI configuration. Shared base searches reduce scheduler load but amplify blast radius: one SPL regression starves dozens of KPIs. Ad-hoc KPI searches without shared bases increase maintenance burden but shrink correlation difficulty during incidents. Threshold caching and adaptive policies add latency between raw breach and surfaced severity; pairing base-search completion timestamps with itsi_summary _time exposes when caches serve stale truth.

Performance impact from leading wildcard clauses, unbounded earliest time, massive by clauses, and expensive joins is well documented in platform tuning guides. Optimisation patterns include narrowing indexes, replacing star prefixes with indexed fields, pre-aggregating into summary indexes, splitting high-fanout entity classes, and staggering cron minutes away from wall-clock boundaries. Search head cluster failover shifts captain responsibilities; scheduler logs continue on members but affinities may move, producing transient duplicate or missing completions until membership stabilizes.

Role-based access for ITSI REST endpoints such as /services/event_management_interface/saved_search_query_metrics and broader /services/itoa_interface routes typically requires elevated capability. Store automation tokens in the credential facility and rotate with platform secrets policy. Read-only inventory exports may be delegated to narrowly scoped roles on some estates.

Risk briefing: index outages, ingestion lag, and asymmetric clock skew between search heads and indexers create apparent freshness gaps without moral failure in KPI logic. Legal reviewers may require masking hostnames in evidence exports. Change windows that pause entity discovery saved searches legitimately spike pseudo rates until rediscovery completes.

Differentiation recap: this control ranks KPI base searches where runtime percentiles, skew versus fleet median, skip or continued scheduler outcomes, pseudo-entity percentages, backfill coverage hints from ITSI daemon lanes, REST query metrics, and freshness lag versus itsi_summary last seen threaten dependent KPI readability. Evidence should tie to Service Analyzer drilldowns, base search editors, and configuration management history for itsi_kpi_base_search.conf and itsi_kpi.conf.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane sched_ok reads index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler with status=success and savedsearch_id matching itsi_kpi_base_search so successful completions contribute run_time and result_count distributions per fifteen-minute bin. Preserve splunk_server as search_head identity. Lane sched_skip mirrors the same savedsearch_id filter with status in skipped or continued to capture deferrals that starve freshness without showing as hard errors.

Lane summary_fresh reads index=itsi_summary for KPI summary events carrying base_search_id or equivalent foreign keys, kpi_id dimensions, entity fields, and severities. Compute distinct KPI counts per base search and pseudo-entity hit rates using conservative string tests on entity keys; tune match clauses to your tenant vocabulary without widening wildcards destructively.

Lane splunkd_itsi reads index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd filtered to component ItsiKpiSearch or ItsiServiceMonitor for narratives mentioning backfill, coverage, gaps, or replay. Derive a backfill coverage style percentage from the ratio of benign to suspicious lines within each bin; calibrate thresholds after a baseline week.

Lane rest_metrics issues rest to /services/event_management_interface/saved_search_query_metrics on the local search head to gather saved search execution latency hints keyed by savedsearch_name. Map names to base_search_id values consistently with inventory.

Maintain lookup table itsi_base_search_inventory.csv keyed by base_search_id with owner_team, business_priority, runbook_url, scheduling_interval_minutes, expected_entity_count, and criticality. Refresh from version control whenever ITSI content packs import new KPI bases. Optional sourcetype itsi:base_search:metadata in itsi_summary can publish last successful metadata rows per base search; if your deployment lacks this sourcetype, the join arm returns empty without breaking the pipeline.

props.conf for scheduler and splunkd should preserve timestamps and avoid truncating long reason fields. Verify _internal retention spans long weekends. Forwarders must include all search head cluster members.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as uc_13_2_5_itsi_kpi_base_search_performance_ranked with a fifteen-minute schedule in steady state, dispatch earliest=-6h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high. Throttle duplicate search_head and base_search_id pairs for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include owner_team, business_priority, and recommended_action tokens in pager bodies with links to Service Analyzer and the KPI base search editor.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans five arms so a single silent lane cannot hide base-search distress. stats merges lanes per bin and key. streamstats applies a twenty-four bin sliding average of run_time_p95_sec by base_search_id to approximate six hours of behavior at fifteen-minute granularity. eventstats adds search_head median context for skew ratio. join enriches governance metadata from itsi_base_search_inventory.csv and optional itsi_summary metadata. case maps combined economics into severity. The closing table lists operational columns for triage.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.2.5 ITSI KPI base search runtime variance backfill pseudo-entity freshness ranked multisearch")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-6h@h latest=now status=success savedsearch_id=*itsi_kpi_base_search*
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,""))))
      | eval base_search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(savedsearch_id,savedsearch_name,"__missing_bs__"))))
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__"
      | eval lane="sched_ok"
      | bin _time span=15m
      | eval run_sec=coalesce(tonumber(run_time),if(isnotnull(run_time_ms),tonumber(run_time_ms)/1000,null()),0)
      | eval res_cnt=tonumber(coalesce(result_count,0))
      | stats count AS ok_runs perc95(run_sec) AS run_time_p95_sec perc50(res_cnt) AS result_count_p50 max(_time) AS last_bs_success BY _time search_head base_search_id kpi_id
      | eval lane="sched_ok" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-6h@h latest=now (status=skipped OR status=continued) savedsearch_id=*itsi_kpi_base_search*
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,""))))
      | eval base_search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(savedsearch_id,savedsearch_name,"__missing_bs__"))))
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__"
      | bin _time span=15m
      | stats count AS skip_cnt values(status) AS skip_status_vals BY _time search_head base_search_id kpi_id
      | eval lane="sched_skip" ]
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval base_search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(base_search_id,itsi_base_search_id,savedsearch_id,"__missing_bs__"))))
      | eval kpi_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,itsi_kpi_id,"__rollup__"))))
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,"itsi_sh_auto"))))
      | eval entity_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(entity_key,entity_id,entity_title,entity,""))))
      | eval pseudo_hit=if(match(entity_key,"pseudo|unknown|__itsi") OR len(entity_key)<2 OR isnull(entity_key),1,0)
      | eval alert_state=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(alert_severity,severity,"normal"))))
      | eval freshness_ts=_time
      | bin _time span=15m
      | stats dc(kpi_id) AS distinct_kpis_dependent sum(pseudo_hit) AS pseudo_hits count AS kpi_evt max(freshness_ts) AS last_kpi_seen BY _time search_head base_search_id
      | eval pseudo_entity_pct=if(kpi_evt>0,round(100*pseudo_hits/kpi_evt,2),0)
      | eval lane="summary_fresh"
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-6h@h latest=now (component=ItsiKpiSearch OR component=ItsiServiceMonitor)
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,""))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?i)base_search_id\\s*=\\s*(?<base_search_id>[^\\s\\|]+)"
      | eval base_search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(base_search_id,"__splunkd_hint__"))))
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__"
      | bin _time span=15m
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | eval backfill_hit=if(match(lm,"backfill|gap|catch.?up|coverage|replay"),1,0)
      | stats sum(backfill_hit) AS backfill_signals count AS splunkd_evt BY _time search_head base_search_id kpi_id
      | eval backfill_coverage_pct=if(splunkd_evt>0,round(100*(1-(backfill_signals/splunkd_evt)),2),100)
      | eval lane="splunkd_itsi" ]
    [ | rest /services/event_management_interface/saved_search_query_metrics splunk_server=local count=0
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,""))))
      | eval base_search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(savedsearch_name,savedsearch_id,"__rest_metrics__"))))
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__"
      | eval _time=now()
      | bin _time span=15m
      | eval rest_latency_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,execution_ms,0))
      | stats avg(rest_latency_ms) AS rest_latency_avg_ms BY _time search_head base_search_id kpi_id
      | eval lane="rest_metrics" ]
| eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(search_head,""))))
| eval base_search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(base_search_id,"__missing_bs__"))))
| eval kpi_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,"__rollup__"))))
| stats max(eval(if(lane=="sched_ok",run_time_p95_sec,null()))) AS run_time_p95_sec max(eval(if(lane=="sched_ok",result_count_p50,null()))) AS result_count_p50 max(eval(if(lane=="sched_ok",last_bs_success,null()))) AS last_bs_success max(eval(if(lane=="sched_ok",ok_runs,null()))) AS ok_runs max(eval(if(lane=="sched_skip",skip_cnt,null()))) AS skip_cnt max(eval(if(lane=="summary_fresh",distinct_kpis_dependent,null()))) AS distinct_kpis_dependent max(eval(if(lane=="summary_fresh",pseudo_entity_pct,null()))) AS pseudo_entity_pct max(eval(if(lane=="summary_fresh",last_kpi_seen,null()))) AS last_kpi_seen max(eval(if(lane=="splunkd_itsi",backfill_coverage_pct,null()))) AS backfill_coverage_pct max(eval(if(lane=="rest_metrics",rest_latency_avg_ms,null()))) AS rest_latency_avg_ms values(lane) AS lanes BY _time search_head base_search_id kpi_id
| sort 0 search_head base_search_id kpi_id + _time
| streamstats window=24 current=t global=f avg(run_time_p95_sec) AS run_time_ma_6h BY base_search_id
| eventstats median(run_time_p95_sec) AS sh_run_median BY search_head
| eval run_time_skew_ratio=if(coalesce(sh_run_median,0)>0.01,round(run_time_p95_sec/sh_run_median,3),0)
| eval freshness_lag_minutes=round((now()-coalesce(last_kpi_seen,last_bs_success,_time))/60,2)
| join type=left max=0 base_search_id
    [| inputlookup itsi_base_search_inventory.csv
     | eval base_search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(base_search_id,bs_id,""))))
     | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team,team,"unowned")))
     | eval business_priority=trim(toString(coalesce(business_priority,priority,"standard")))
     | eval scheduling_interval_minutes=tonumber(coalesce(scheduling_interval_minutes,5))
     | fields base_search_id owner_team business_priority runbook_url scheduling_interval_minutes expected_entity_count criticality ]
| join type=left max=0 base_search_id
    [ search index=itsi_summary sourcetype=itsi:base_search:metadata earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval base_search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(base_search_id,bs_id,""))))
      | dedup base_search_id ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eval business_priority=coalesce(business_priority,"standard")
| eval kpi_id=coalesce(kpi_id,"__rollup__")
| eval interval_sec=coalesce(scheduling_interval_minutes,5)*60
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(run_time_p95_sec,0)>(interval_sec*0.8) OR coalesce(freshness_lag_minutes,0)>15 OR coalesce(pseudo_entity_pct,0)>25,"critical",
    coalesce(skip_cnt,0)>5 OR coalesce(freshness_lag_minutes,0)>10 OR coalesce(run_time_skew_ratio,0)>2.5,"high",
    coalesce(run_time_p95_sec,0)>(interval_sec*0.5) OR coalesce(pseudo_entity_pct,0)>10 OR coalesce(backfill_coverage_pct,100)<85,"medium",
    true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(run_time_p95_sec,0)>(interval_sec*0.8),"resize_base_search_scope_fix_entity_fanout_review_itsi_kpi_base_search_conf",
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(freshness_lag_minutes,0)>15,"repair_summary_freshness_align_scheduler_cron_validate_itsi_summary_ingest_lag",
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(pseudo_entity_pct,0)>25,"remediate_entity_discovery_lookups_rebind_entity_id_fields_drill_service_analyzer",
    severity=="high","tune_shared_base_search_split_kpis_validate_metric_qualifier_threshold_cache",
    severity=="medium","optimize_spl_remove_leading_wildcards_schedule_stagger_itoa_rest_inventory",
    true(),"continue_monitoring_baseline_run_time_and_pseudo_entity_rate")
| table _time search_head base_search_id owner_team business_priority run_time_p95_sec freshness_lag_minutes pseudo_entity_pct backfill_coverage_pct distinct_kpis_dependent severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[uc_13_2_5_itsi_kpi_base_search_performance_ranked_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -6h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch uc_13_2_5_itsi_kpi_base_search_performance_ranked | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance guidance: if Job Inspector queue time exceeds internal objectives, materialize each multisearch arm hourly into a summary index keyed by _time, search_head, and base_search_id, widen operational alerts to read summaries, and retain this search for investigations.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the ITSI search head: compare scheduler.log completions for a known KPI base search to Splunk rows in sched_ok for the same wall clock fifteen-minute bin. Confirm run_time percentiles move in the same direction as Job Inspector durations for representative jobs.

Validate freshness_lag_minutes by comparing last_kpi_seen in itsi_summary to Service Analyzer last refresh narratives for KPIs tied to the same base_search_id. Induce a controlled delay in a lab by widening earliest bounds temporarily and confirm the alert surfaces within two windows.

Validate pseudo_entity_pct by forcing entity discovery to return ambiguous keys in a sandbox service; confirm pseudo_hit logic rises and recommended_action points to entity remediation. Restore discovery and confirm decay.

Validate inventory joins: run inputlookup itsi_base_search_inventory.csv alone and confirm base_search_id coverage matches production KPI bases. Join misses should yield unowned owner_team until governance catches up.

REST arm validation: execute the same rest path manually with a session key and compare savedsearch_name keys to inventory. Empty rest arms during credential rotation should downgrade rest_latency contribution without crashing the search.

Negative validation: after recovery, when run_time_p95_sec falls below half the interval budget, skip_cnt stays zero, pseudo_entity_pct falls under five percent, and freshness_lag_minutes stays under five, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for the canary base search during four consecutive runs.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — A base search binds too many entities after an automatic discovery import: pseudo_entity_pct spikes, run_time_p95_sec crosses eighty percent of the interval budget, and dependent KPIs read not available; shrink entity filters, split bases, and replay discovery with caps.

Case 2 — Entity discovery lookup goes stale after configuration management retired a join column: entity_id extraction yields blank keys; refresh lookup generation, fix fieldaliases, and rebind kpi_id metric qualifiers.

Case 3 — SPL regression after a release widens a leading wildcard: runtime skew ratio jumps against search_head median; roll forward a hotfix search string and add code review gates for KPI bases.

Case 4 — Missing Common Information Model acceleration for an index referenced only by this base slows joins: restore acceleration or move pre-aggregation upstream; validate summaries separately.

Case 5 — Entity ID field renamed in upstream logs without updating KPI configuration: pseudo-entity rate climbs; align itsi_kpi.conf entity fields and validate with service template tests.

Case 6 — Search head cluster failover during maintenance duplicates or misses completions: correlate skip_cnt with UC-13.1.10 captain signals; pause pages until cluster stabilizes if change metadata explains the burst.

Case 7 — Index outage on a dependency index blocks base completion: freshness_lag_minutes grows while scheduler still marks continued; route to indexer health before blaming ITSI logic.

Case 8 — Scheduling cron misaligned to five-minute KPI expectations after template edit: interval_sec mismatch makes severity look critical; fix scheduling_interval_minutes in inventory and itsi_kpi_base_search.conf cadence together.

Case 9 — KPI threshold policy changed during incident response: downstream severities flip without base-search movement; annotate change tickets so this alert is not misinterpreted as runtime regression.

Case 10 — Team disabled an entity discovery saved search during flood control: pseudo entities appear; restore discovery with throttled batches and coordinate with UC-13.1.5 skip analytics.

Case 11 — ITSI app upgrade rewrites base-search SPL silently via content pack: compare configuration management diffs, roll back if unintended, and replay validation cases.

Case 12 — New KPI added to a shared base blows up cardinality: distinct_kpis_dependent rises while runtime spikes; split the new KPI into a dedicated base with its own schedule.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.2.5 ITSI KPI base search runtime variance backfill pseudo-entity freshness ranked multisearch")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-6h@h latest=now status=success savedsearch_id=*itsi_kpi_base_search*
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,""))))
      | eval base_search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(savedsearch_id,savedsearch_name,"__missing_bs__"))))
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__"
      | eval lane="sched_ok"
      | bin _time span=15m
      | eval run_sec=coalesce(tonumber(run_time),if(isnotnull(run_time_ms),tonumber(run_time_ms)/1000,null()),0)
      | eval res_cnt=tonumber(coalesce(result_count,0))
      | stats count AS ok_runs perc95(run_sec) AS run_time_p95_sec perc50(res_cnt) AS result_count_p50 max(_time) AS last_bs_success BY _time search_head base_search_id kpi_id
      | eval lane="sched_ok" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-6h@h latest=now (status=skipped OR status=continued) savedsearch_id=*itsi_kpi_base_search*
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,""))))
      | eval base_search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(savedsearch_id,savedsearch_name,"__missing_bs__"))))
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__"
      | bin _time span=15m
      | stats count AS skip_cnt values(status) AS skip_status_vals BY _time search_head base_search_id kpi_id
      | eval lane="sched_skip" ]
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval base_search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(base_search_id,itsi_base_search_id,savedsearch_id,"__missing_bs__"))))
      | eval kpi_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,itsi_kpi_id,"__rollup__"))))
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,"itsi_sh_auto"))))
      | eval entity_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(entity_key,entity_id,entity_title,entity,""))))
      | eval pseudo_hit=if(match(entity_key,"pseudo|unknown|__itsi") OR len(entity_key)<2 OR isnull(entity_key),1,0)
      | eval alert_state=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(alert_severity,severity,"normal"))))
      | eval freshness_ts=_time
      | bin _time span=15m
      | stats dc(kpi_id) AS distinct_kpis_dependent sum(pseudo_hit) AS pseudo_hits count AS kpi_evt max(freshness_ts) AS last_kpi_seen BY _time search_head base_search_id
      | eval pseudo_entity_pct=if(kpi_evt>0,round(100*pseudo_hits/kpi_evt,2),0)
      | eval lane="summary_fresh"
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-6h@h latest=now (component=ItsiKpiSearch OR component=ItsiServiceMonitor)
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,""))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?i)base_search_id\\s*=\\s*(?<base_search_id>[^\\s\\|]+)"
      | eval base_search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(base_search_id,"__splunkd_hint__"))))
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__"
      | bin _time span=15m
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | eval backfill_hit=if(match(lm,"backfill|gap|catch.?up|coverage|replay"),1,0)
      | stats sum(backfill_hit) AS backfill_signals count AS splunkd_evt BY _time search_head base_search_id kpi_id
      | eval backfill_coverage_pct=if(splunkd_evt>0,round(100*(1-(backfill_signals/splunkd_evt)),2),100)
      | eval lane="splunkd_itsi" ]
    [ | rest /services/event_management_interface/saved_search_query_metrics splunk_server=local count=0
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,""))))
      | eval base_search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(savedsearch_name,savedsearch_id,"__rest_metrics__"))))
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__"
      | eval _time=now()
      | bin _time span=15m
      | eval rest_latency_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,execution_ms,0))
      | stats avg(rest_latency_ms) AS rest_latency_avg_ms BY _time search_head base_search_id kpi_id
      | eval lane="rest_metrics" ]
| eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(search_head,""))))
| eval base_search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(base_search_id,"__missing_bs__"))))
| eval kpi_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,"__rollup__"))))
| stats max(eval(if(lane=="sched_ok",run_time_p95_sec,null()))) AS run_time_p95_sec max(eval(if(lane=="sched_ok",result_count_p50,null()))) AS result_count_p50 max(eval(if(lane=="sched_ok",last_bs_success,null()))) AS last_bs_success max(eval(if(lane=="sched_ok",ok_runs,null()))) AS ok_runs max(eval(if(lane=="sched_skip",skip_cnt,null()))) AS skip_cnt max(eval(if(lane=="summary_fresh",distinct_kpis_dependent,null()))) AS distinct_kpis_dependent max(eval(if(lane=="summary_fresh",pseudo_entity_pct,null()))) AS pseudo_entity_pct max(eval(if(lane=="summary_fresh",last_kpi_seen,null()))) AS last_kpi_seen max(eval(if(lane=="splunkd_itsi",backfill_coverage_pct,null()))) AS backfill_coverage_pct max(eval(if(lane=="rest_metrics",rest_latency_avg_ms,null()))) AS rest_latency_avg_ms values(lane) AS lanes BY _time search_head base_search_id kpi_id
| sort 0 search_head base_search_id kpi_id + _time
| streamstats window=24 current=t global=f avg(run_time_p95_sec) AS run_time_ma_6h BY base_search_id
| eventstats median(run_time_p95_sec) AS sh_run_median BY search_head
| eval run_time_skew_ratio=if(coalesce(sh_run_median,0)>0.01,round(run_time_p95_sec/sh_run_median,3),0)
| eval freshness_lag_minutes=round((now()-coalesce(last_kpi_seen,last_bs_success,_time))/60,2)
| join type=left max=0 base_search_id
    [| inputlookup itsi_base_search_inventory.csv
     | eval base_search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(base_search_id,bs_id,""))))
     | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team,team,"unowned")))
     | eval business_priority=trim(toString(coalesce(business_priority,priority,"standard")))
     | eval scheduling_interval_minutes=tonumber(coalesce(scheduling_interval_minutes,5))
     | fields base_search_id owner_team business_priority runbook_url scheduling_interval_minutes expected_entity_count criticality ]
| join type=left max=0 base_search_id
    [ search index=itsi_summary sourcetype=itsi:base_search:metadata earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval base_search_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(base_search_id,bs_id,""))))
      | dedup base_search_id ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eval business_priority=coalesce(business_priority,"standard")
| eval kpi_id=coalesce(kpi_id,"__rollup__")
| eval interval_sec=coalesce(scheduling_interval_minutes,5)*60
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(run_time_p95_sec,0)>(interval_sec*0.8) OR coalesce(freshness_lag_minutes,0)>15 OR coalesce(pseudo_entity_pct,0)>25,"critical",
    coalesce(skip_cnt,0)>5 OR coalesce(freshness_lag_minutes,0)>10 OR coalesce(run_time_skew_ratio,0)>2.5,"high",
    coalesce(run_time_p95_sec,0)>(interval_sec*0.5) OR coalesce(pseudo_entity_pct,0)>10 OR coalesce(backfill_coverage_pct,100)<85,"medium",
    true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(run_time_p95_sec,0)>(interval_sec*0.8),"resize_base_search_scope_fix_entity_fanout_review_itsi_kpi_base_search_conf",
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(freshness_lag_minutes,0)>15,"repair_summary_freshness_align_scheduler_cron_validate_itsi_summary_ingest_lag",
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(pseudo_entity_pct,0)>25,"remediate_entity_discovery_lookups_rebind_entity_id_fields_drill_service_analyzer",
    severity=="high","tune_shared_base_search_split_kpis_validate_metric_qualifier_threshold_cache",
    severity=="medium","optimize_spl_remove_leading_wildcards_schedule_stagger_itoa_rest_inventory",
    true(),"continue_monitoring_baseline_run_time_and_pseudo_entity_rate")
| table _time search_head base_search_id owner_team business_priority run_time_p95_sec freshness_lag_minutes pseudo_entity_pct backfill_coverage_pct distinct_kpis_dependent severity recommended_action
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_pct) AS cpu_pct latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-6h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS search_head
| join type=left max=0 search_head [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Inventory.os) AS os FROM datamodel=Inventory WHERE nodename=Inventory earliest=-6h@h latest=now BY Inventory.dest
| rename Inventory.dest AS search_head ]
| table search_head cpu_pct mem_used os
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by search_head and base_search_id with drilldowns to scheduler events, itsi_summary samples, splunkd ITSI components, and Service Analyzer deep links; single-value tiles for freshness_lag_minutes and pseudo_entity_pct; timechart of run_time_p95_sec with six-hour moving average overlay.

## Known False Positives

Planned base-search redesign windows intentionally widen earliest bounds and can inflate freshness_lag_minutes until backfill completes; annotate maintenance metadata before paging. Mass entity import projects and rediscovery batches temporarily elevate pseudo_entity_pct while lookups catch up; downgrade severity when change tickets document the import. Scheduled lookup refresh jobs that rewrite entity tables during business hours can mimic discovery failure for one or two intervals; validate job success before opening incidents. Throttled disaster recovery sites that stretch search latency can push run_time_p95_sec high without primary-region logic errors; compare environment labels in inventory. Change-window pauses that disable KPI bases under approval should suppress alerts via suppression rules keyed on change_ticket_id rather than muting the control globally. Nightly backup intervals that pause indexer responsiveness can drive continued scheduler statuses; pair with indexer health UCs. Content-pack staging clusters with tiny data volumes produce unstable percentiles; gate production alerts by business_priority. REST credential rotation empties the saved_search_query_metrics arm briefly; expect single-window info severity only. Executive drilldown filters that hide entity rows can look like pseudo-entity surges when analysts compare unlike scopes. Finally, clock skew between search heads and indexers creates artificial freshness_lag_minutes until time hygiene UCs clear the drift.
